decks.label: Brineward Outlast (boss2)
decks.notes: Open Mireglide (1m armor+poison+haste) and Tidefoot Guard (1m 12armor+poison) to survive iron_golem's 10dmg/2.5s while seeding poison; Mire Bloom (1m) is cheap filler poison; Bog Catalyst (1s+1m) doubles the stack to the cap; Stagnant Bulwark (1s+1m) lays a poison-over-time field behind 10 armor; Brineward (2m 14armor+heal12+poison) is the outlast anchor; Drowning Lance detonates half the pool for big pierce; Brine Bedrock & Mistplate top up armor/heal/poison. Cheap 1m cards front-loaded so the deck always opens; only two 2m cards and they sit late so mana has regenerated.

decks.label: Mistplate Slow-Wall (boss2)
decks.notes: Variant that adds slow throttle to the armor wall. Stormsplash (1m poison+slow+burn) and Mistplate (2m armor+poison+slow) cut the golem's attack cadence while poison ticks; Bog Catalyst doubles; Marsh Squall is the AoE poison detonator; Bogplate (1s+1m vengeance poison) and Brineward backstop armor+heal. Order keeps all the 1m appliers up front before the 2m anchors.

decks.label: Bog Catalyst Stackwall (boss3)
decks.notes: Vs lizard_king (enrages <30% HP, multi-hit) the plan is to race poison to the cap fast and detonate before enrage. Mireglide + Tidefoot Guard open armor+poison; Alchemic Drain (1m poison+heal6) is cheap sustain; Bog Catalyst doubles the stack; Stagnant Bulwark drips poison behind armor; Drowning Lance is the big detonator; Brineward/Brine Bedrock/Mistplate/Bogplate keep the wall up. Detonating poison (pierce) bypasses the dragon's def 3 and ends the fight before enrage scaling matters.

decks.label: Steam Bulwark Brace (boss3)
decks.notes: Poison+burn+block hybrid. Boilstep (1m burn+poison) and Steam Bulwark (2m armor + brace poison+burn) add a burn layer on top of poison so both DoTs tick; Bog Catalyst doubles poison; Drowning Lance detonates. Cheap 1m appliers lead; the two 2m walls (Brineward, Steam Bulwark) and Bogplate sit after mana has banked.

decks.label: Drain Wall vs Bog Witch (boss4)
decks.notes: Bog_witch drains 15% and only hits for 7, so the danger is the timeout, not death. Poison ignores armor AND can't be drained back, so this maxes poison throughput: Mireglide/Tidefoot open, Alchemic Drain cheap sustain, Bog Catalyst doubles, Stagnant Bulwark drips, then Drowning Lance + Marsh Squall give TWO detonators so the chunk-capped pool converts to burst pierce and actually closes the fight. Brineward/Brine Bedrock heal through the witch's chip damage.

decks.label: Twin-Detonator Race (boss4)
decks.notes: Leaner, more aggressive poison build to beat the witch's heal-race. Slipstream (1m pierce+poison) and Venom Dance (1s 3/+5 poison on vengeance) ramp the stack fast; Bog Catalyst doubles; Drowning Lance + Marsh Squall are the two detonators; Stagnant Bulwark and Brineward provide the only armor/heal so it must close quickly. Front-loaded 1m/1s appliers keep tempo high.

decks.label: Endgame Poison Fortress (boss5)
decks.notes: Tuned vs desert_golem (450 HP, def 4, 10dmg/2.7s, +10 shield/7s). Two openers of cheap armor+poison (Mireglide, Tidefoot Guard), Alchemic Drain sustain, Bog Catalyst + Slipvenom Tempo to spike and re-spike the stack past 10, Stagnant Bulwark drip, then Drowning Lance + Marsh Squall detonators to punch through the golem's def/shield with pierce. Brineward/Brine Bedrock heal the long fight. Order keeps all 1m appliers ahead of the 2m walls.

decks.label: Shield-Pierce Poison (boss5)
decks.notes: Maximizes pierce-detonation against desert_golem's def 4 + periodic shield, which blunts ordinary armor-based damage. Slipvenom Tempo big poison spike, Bog Catalyst doubler, then Drowning Lance + Marsh Squall convert the capped pool to pierce that ignores def/shield. Magma Welling and Stagnant Bulwark/Brineward/Mistplate hold the wall. 1m appliers lead, 2m anchors trail.
